## Review narrative

The canonical review context at kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/review-contexts/8833309f76affc01695ba75b79a525b6a6c183259ae6db69b00b79f04b75a6ad.json was read first; its validation_obligations list is empty, review_phase is discovery, prior_findings is empty, and the inherited frontier is none. The candidate delta was pinned mechanically: n5's barrier base is commit 2dc6c8beb4a1 (matching the evidence nonce) and n6's base is d2cc7441ad15; git diff between them touches exactly the two agent profiles outside workflow state, and the current worktree is byte-identical to n6's base outside kaola-workflow/. The six workflow-planner/contractor tomls, all five contract validators, test-opencode-edition.js, and test-kimi-edition.js are byte-unchanged across the n5 window, confirming n5's zero-pins-narrowed and zero-toml-edits claims by diff rather than trust.

Re-run outputs (real exit codes or terminal success sentinels captured, from the worktree): root validate-workflow-contracts.js exit 0; codex validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js exit 0; gitlab and gitea validators exit 0; test-agent-profile-parity.js passed 458 assertions; test-route-reachability.js passed 2276; test-bash-block-guards.js passed all 19 (Tests A and D extract the contractor Step-8a block by the "git status --porcelain | while" marker and execute it in a scratch git repo, including the renamed-tracked-file and no-plan scenarios); validate-vendored-agents.js passed 16 agents; simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js exit 0 with "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed". The byte-twin pair cmp is clean (scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js and the plugins/kaola-workflow copy are byte-identical); the plugin copy still fails when executed standalone because it resolves root to plugins/kaola-workflow/, which ships no commands/ tree — that failure mode is structural, identical at n5's base (file and tree both unchanged), and the npm chains never execute that copy standalone, so root-execution-green plus byte-identity is the correct evidence for the twin. test-opencode-edition.js exit 1 with exactly the base failure-set (383 passed, one pre-existing H1/#F3 hookPath failure, and critically no assertion-A Claude-path leak); test-kimi-edition.js exit 1 with the identical pre-existing ENOENT FATAL on hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh, a file outside n5's delta. No new failure in either additive suite.

Planner-briefed-only walk from the 249-line profile alone: a fresh planner would claim correctly (Method step 1 carries the exact startup command with --workflow-path adaptive required, --attest-planner-spawn, and the repo-root-not-worktree posture), refuse correctly (any claim_verdict not acquired/owned returns verbatim with the structural refuse-vs-escalate classification by result including target_set_mismatch; the frozen-plan overwrite guard stops on the plan_hash marker with overwrite permitted only inside the validator-repair loop; a pre-authored Nodes table or AUTHOR EXACTLY outside the bounded unfrozen-plan repair loop returns planner_control_boundary_violation), author complete plans (the grammar section keeps roles, three shapes, FANOUT_CAP as runtime-not-width, exact-file write sets, G1/G2/G3 gate walls, upstream-provisioned gate instrumentation, tdd-guide vs implementer with non_tdd_reason, canonical ledger header, briefs syntax, compact-plan posture, generated_port_split, model tiers, and the full wait-budget contract; the write-set completeness bullet retains all five overflow classes, the four-tree symbol grep, the registration surface, and the accumulated-root-diff spec for forge ports), freeze mechanically (self-check then handoff, no authoring-allowed, bundle_state_incoherent verbatim), and return the four typed packets with their field names. The re-plan dispatch mode retains every T17 profileToken (verified live by route-reachability's 2276 green assertions and spot-checked in the file): entry condition, no claim/startup, frozen-parent immutability, the eight packet integrity fields, the seeded workflow-plan.next.md target, lineage and obligation carry-over, dispatch-log plus attestation provenance, replan_planner_attestation_invalid, the bounded child-repair loop, and main-never-repairs-the-child.

Contractor seam walk: the hard boundary (never dispatch, never judge, never ask, Sonnet-pinned, never a gate, never remediate) survives as bullets; the finalize command's dispatch handle at commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md lines 324-341 promises Step 8a, 8b, 7, 8, 8c, kaola_script/CLAIM_JS re-derivation, and SINK_KIND/SINK_ISSUE_ACTION re-read, and every promise resolves against the trimmed profile in the correct choreography order (8a before staging, 8b after 8a and before Step 8's commit, Step 7 staging-only, 8c verify-or-fail-closed with the three precedence-ordered typed reasons plus the consumer final-validation.md path, 8c.2 gap sweep, Step 8 committed-work-only). The attestation boundary is intact on both sides: the profile states only the genuinely-dispatched contractor passes --attest-contractor-spawn, never the main session inline. The three-section output contract is verbatim-shaped.

Cut-material enforcement audit: every removed planner block is either restated tersely or owned by a typed refusal at freeze or runtime — ledger aliases by ledger_header_invalid, brief id errors by brief_unknown_node/brief_duplicate_node, directory tokens at freeze and write_set_granularity at the barrier, aggregator splits by generated_port_split, hand-added annotations by invalid_annotation, model tokens by model_invalid, decision ids by decision_id_conflict, agent-set additions by the agent-registration gap refusal (removals keep the manual-surface warning in prose), speculation eligibility by the mechanical validator/runtime derivation, and re-plan provenance by replan_planner_attestation_invalid. The removed attestation-digest computation detail is fail-closed behind that same typed refusal. The question-shaped section's cut guardrails live pinned on the six adapt surfaces (the root validator's adaptSurfaces block asserts them there, not on the planner profile). Contractor cuts are script-owned mechanism narration: cmdFinalize's internal stamping, the consumer hash refusals (final_validation_unbound/stale enforced inside --finalize-check), the sink-merge state-present refuse, and the main-worktree cleanup atomicity all execute in the scripts, and the typed-status-never-free-text rule survives tersely. The only pure-guidance losses (cardinality-count note, doc-updater-before-finalize suggestion) are advisory with no failure mode reachable through a gate.

Pin and needle survival: the codex dual md/toml loop (validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js lines 891-895: planner_override, difficulty alone is not evidence, never inflate a budget to hide a wedged agent, asserted against both agents/workflow-planner.md and the toml) resolves; the gitlab/gitea assertConcept blocks over agents/workflow-planner.md resolve; the root validator's contractor pins (--attest-contractor-spawn, kaola-workflow-ledger-compare.js, sync worktree->main FIRST) all live inside the surviving Step-8a executable lines; the twelve HARD parity tokens are unconditional against the md and all four toml trees and are green; the reviewer-contract-v2-authoring PIN block is verbatim. The parity count drop from 461 to 458 is the md-to-toml conditional direction shedding obligations with trimmed prose, which is the contract working as designed. Toml parity: all six tomls byte-unchanged and both triplets cmp byte-identical across the three editions, so the gate claim's toml condition holds both vacuously and factually. Provenance sweep: a grep for issue refs, decision ids, ADR citations, and issue-N tokens over both profiles returns zero matches, and n5's delta touches no other prompt surface. The in-node transform repair is verified: the canonical parenthetical "(prefer `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts`, then `$HOME/.claude/kaola-workflow/scripts`, then `./scripts`)" is present in both profiles (line-wrapped), and the strip regex at scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js line 258 uses whitespace-class separators so the wrap matches — confirmed operationally by the opencode suite returning to its exact base failure-set with no Claude-path leak.

Judgment on the contractor miss reason: honest. The 208-line floor arithmetic checks out against the file — 14 lines of frontmatter plus managed marker, 10 of Prompt Defense Baseline, the 38-fence-line Step-8a block that test-bash-block-guards.js executes verbatim in two scenarios (its surviving comments state parsing constraints the executed while-loop depends on, and the pinned needles live in its executable lines, so compressing it would change guard-executed semantics with the guard itself out of the write set), roughly 15 lines of required output contract, and the roughly 90-line delegated procedure that commands/kaola-workflow-finalize.md names as living exclusively in this profile — a contract the root, gitlab, and gitea validators pin from both sides. Cutting to 150 would gut the sole-home body or move it back into the command against the pinned relocation. The 249-line planner hit its target legitimately.

review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: The n5 front-agents diet is certified clean: both trimmed profiles preserve every Band-1 invariant a future planner or contractor would need, all pins and needles resolve against the surviving prose, the tomls and validators are byte-untouched with both edition triplets identical, the guard-executed Step-8a block behaves verbatim, the additive suites match their base failure-sets exactly, and the contractor's documented 208-line floor is an honest, validator-pinned minimum.
